system_prompt:
You are Hermes Agent, an intelligent AI assistant created by Nous Research. You are helpful, knowledgeable, and direct. You assist users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via your tools. You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

Conversation started: Saturday, April 04, 2026 05:04 PM
Model: claude-opus-4-6
Provider: anthropic

You are on a text messaging communication platform, Telegram. Please do not use markdown as it does not render. You can send media files natively: to deliver a file to the user, include MEDIA:/absolute/path/to/file in your response. Images (.png, .jpg, .webp) appear as photos, audio (.ogg) sends as voice bubbles, and videos (.mp4) play inline. You can also include image URLs in markdown format ![alt](url) and they will be sent as native photos.

function.name: web_extract
function.description: Extract content from web page URLs. Returns page content in markdown format. Also works with PDF URLs (arxiv papers, documents, etc.) — pass the PDF link directly and it converts to markdown text. Pages under 5000 chars return full markdown; larger pages are LLM-summarized and capped at ~5000 chars per page. Pages over 2M chars are refused. If a URL fails or times out, use the browser tool to access it instead.

urls.description: List of URLs to extract content from (max 5 URLs per call)

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the web for information on any topic. Returns up to 5 relevant results with titles, URLs, and descriptions.

query.description: The search query to look up on the web

messages.content: Research how the Hermes Agent community uses, deploys, and improves Hermes. Check the GitHub repo (NousResearch/hermes-agent), Discord, recent commits, issues, PRs, discussions, and any community content. Focus on: what people deploy it on, what upgrades/plugins they add, what workflows work best, common setups, and community contributions. Return a concise summary.
I'll research the Hermes Agent community thoroughly. Let me start by searching for the GitHub repo and community content.

Unfortunately, both my web search and web extraction tools are experiencing authentication errors (401 Unauthorized) with the underlying Tavily API. This means I cannot access GitHub, Discord, or any web content to research the Hermes Agent community right now.

Here is my summary based on the task attempt:

**What I did:**
- Attempted multiple web searches for "NousResearch hermes-agent" on GitHub, Discord, and community content
- Attempted direct URL extraction from github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent, its issues page, and PRs page
- All attempts failed with 401 Unauthorized errors from the Tavily API backend

**What I found:**
- Unable to retrieve any data due to API authentication failures on all tool calls

**Files created or modified:** None

**Issues encountered:**
- Both `mcp_web_search` and `mcp_web_extract` tools are returning 401 Unauthorized errors from the Tavily API, making it impossible to research any web content at this time

**What I can share from general knowledge:**
- NousResearch is known for their Hermes family of fine-tuned LLMs (Hermes 2, Hermes 3, etc.) built on models like Llama and Mistral
- The "hermes-agent" repo specifically may be a newer project or may exist under a slightly different name
- NousResearch has an active Discord community where users discuss deployments, fine-tuning, and integrations
- Common deployment patterns for NousResearch models include: local inference via llama.cpp/Ollama/vLLM, cloud GPU instances (RunPod, Vast.ai), and integration with agent frameworks
- The community frequently contributes quantized versions (GGUF, GPTQ, AWQ) and shares prompt templates optimized for Hermes's function-calling and tool-use capabilities

I recommend retrying this research task when the API tools are functioning, or using a browser tool if one is available.
